summarize Shrapnel from an Iranian missile struck a facility in Neot Hovav, a major industrial zone known for its concentration of chemical plants, located in the Negev desert in southern Israel on Sunday. The hit triggered a hazardous materials incident and prompted emergency measures, local authorities said, adding that at least one person has been injured and workers from adjoining factories have been evacuated.
tool_input: Shrapnel from an Iranian missile struck a facility in Neot Hovav, a major industrial zone known for its concentration of chemical plants, located in the Negev desert in southern Israel on Sunday. The hit triggered a hazardous materials incident and prompted emergency measures, local authorities said, adding that at least one person has been injured and workers from adjoining factories have been evacuated.
output: Iranian missile shrapnel struck a facility in Neot Hovav, an industrial zone in southern Israel, on Sunday, causing a hazardous materials incident, injuring one person, and prompting evacuations.
